response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong web and full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which is entirely absent from his profile, making him ineligible regardless of technical fit. Beyond that hard disqualifier, his technical background is centered on TypeScript/Node.js, web platforms, and cloud-native development — not C-based embedded firmware, Linux kernel internals, or network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). His exposure to C is minimal and dated (NI LabWindows, a 2015 thesis project), and there is no evidence of L2/L3 networking, switch architecture, or embedded device security experience.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across embedded, web, and systems domains demonstrates adaptability
Prior C/C++ work at National Instruments and in thesis project shows at least foundational exposure to lower-level programming
Mentioned embedded systems in self-description and has worked with Linux environments across multiple roles
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — this is a hard non-negotiable requirement for the role
No experience with network security protocols, L2/L3 networking, MACsec, 802.1X, or any LAN-switch domain knowledge
C programming experience is minimal and largely historical; primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js with no Linux kernel development evidence

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security protocol experience
